NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF
MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES FILED BY DEFENDANT KIMELMAN, ALAN M.D. HEARING SET FOR AUG-07-2007 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ALAN KIMELMAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DR. KIMELMAN'S DEMURRER IS OVERRULED; ANSWER TO BE FILED BY AUGUST 21, 2007. THE DECISION IN YOUNAN V. EQUIFAX, INC. (1980) 111 CAL.APP.3D 498 ADDRESSES THE POINTS RAISED AND REJECTS THE ARGUMENTS MADE IN SUPPORT OF THE DEMURRER. THE MEDICAL REPORT IN HARRIS V. KING (1998) 60 CAL.APP.4TH 1185 WAS A PART OF THE EVIDENCE PRESENTED IN THE ADMINISTRATIVE HEARING AND THAT IS WHY THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE WAS APPLICABLE. HERE, DR. KIMELMAN'S REPORT PREDATES THE LITIGATION AND IS SAID TO BE A PART OF A CONSPIRACY TO DEPRIVE PLAINTIFF OF INSURANCE BENEFITS. (COMPLAINT 21-22 AND 38-40.) AT BEST, IT IS A QUESTION OF FACT AS TO WHETHER THE REPORT IS PROTECTED BY THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE AND AS SUCH, THIS IS NOT A BASIS FOR A DEMURRER. SEE, ACTION APARTMENT ASS'N INC. V. CITY OF SANTA MONICA (2007) 2007 WL 2199003, PART B. THE DECISION IN YOUMAN ESTABLISHES THAT PLAINTIFF HAS PLEAD CAUSES OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION, INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, INDUCING BREACH OF CONTRACT AND INTERFERENCE WITH PROSPECTIVE ADVANTAGE. MR. MANN TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: JOE VICKSTEIN, CSR #4780

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-07-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Defendant Alan Kimelman's Demurrer To Complaint.
Appearances: Lawrence Mann, Esquire (Bourhis & Mann, 415.392.4660) Appearing For Plaintiff. Jennifer M. Ellis, Esquire (The Goldman Law Firm, 415.435.5500) Appearing For Defendant Dr. Alan Kimelman.
Ruling: Argued And The Court Adopts And Modifies Its Tentative Ruling: Hearing Required. Dr. Kimelman's Demurrer Is Overruled; Answer To Be Filed By August 21, 2007.
The Decision In Younan V. Equifax, Inc. (1980) 111 Cal.App.3d 498 Addresses The Points Raised And Rejects The Arguments Made In Support Of The Demurrer. The Medical Report In Harris V. King (1998) 60 Cal.App.4th 1185 Was A Part Of The Evidence Presented In The Administrative Hearing And That Is Why The Litigation Privilege Was Applicable. Here, Dr. Kimelman's Report Predates The Litigation And Is Said To Be A Part Of A Conspiracy To Deprive Plaintiff Of Insurance Benefits. (Complaint 21-22 And 38-40.) At Best, It Is A Question Of Fact As To Whether The Report Is Protected By The Litigation Privilege And As Such, This Is Not A Basis For A Demurrer. See, Action Apartment Ass'n Inc. V. City Of Santa Monica (2007) 2007 Wl 2199003, Part B.
The Decision In Youman Establishes That Plaintiff Has Plead Causes Of Action For Intentional Misrepresentation, Intentional Infliction Of Emotional Distress, Inducing Breach Of Contract And Interference With Prospective Advantage. Mr. Mann To Submit A Form Of Order. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Joe Vickstein, Csr 4780
